Ad Text:
Company Description

Nine is Australia’s largest locally owned media company. Working at Nine, you’ll have access to a unique range of experiences and opportunities, helping drive the success of the country’s most trusted television, radio, digital and publishing brands. 

Our content reaches almost every Australian - meaning what we do has real impact. We bring people together, celebrate the big moments, and capture the everyday ones.

Some of our most beloved brands have been part of Australian life for generations, and others - new on the scene - have already found their place firmly in our lives. We’re evolving and we need people like you to bring new ideas, innovate and make your mark.

Job Description

At Nine, we are harnessing the power of Artificial Intelligence to revolutionise our audience engagement and commercial strategies. As Head of AI Product, you will be at the forefront of this transformation, driving the development and implementation of innovative AI-powered products across our diverse portfolio.

This role is pivotal in shaping Nine’s AI strategy, translating complex AI capabilities into tangible product features that deliver exceptional value to our audiences, advertisers, and internal teams. You will be responsible for defining the product vision, building the roadmap, and leading a cross-functional team to deliver cutting-edge AI solutions.

Reporting to the Director, AI and Data Product, you will work closely with data scientists, engineers, and business stakeholders to identify and capitalise on opportunities to leverage AI in our media landscape.

As Head of AI Product, you will:

Define and champion the AI product vision and strategy: Align AI initiatives with Nine’s overall business objectives, identifying key opportunities to leverage AI for innovation and competitive advantage.

Lead the development of AI-powered products: Drive the end-to-end product lifecycle, from ideation and prototyping to launch and iteration, ensuring products are aligned with user needs and deliver measurable value.

Collaborate with cross-functional teams: Work closely with data scientists, engineers, designers, and business stakeholders to define requirements, prioritise features, and deliver high-quality AI solutions.

Build and maintain the AI product roadmap: Prioritise initiatives based on business impact, technical feasibility, and customer feedback, ensuring a clear and achievable roadmap.

Drive AI adoption across the organization: Evangelise the potential of AI, providing guidance and resources to teams across Nine to explore and integrate AI capabilities into their workflows.

Implement AI governance and ethical practices: Ensure AI products are developed and deployed responsibly, adhering to ethical guidelines and data privacy regulations

Analyse and measure product performance: Define and track key metrics to evaluate the success of AI products, using data-driven insights to inform product decisions and drive continuous improvement.

Stay ahead of the curve: Continuously research and evaluate emerging AI technologies and trends, identifying opportunities to incorporate them into Nine’s product strategy.

Qualifications

We are seeking a passionate and driven product leader with a strong understanding of AI and its potential to transform the media industry. You will have a proven track record of delivering successful AI products and a deep understanding of the product development lifecycle.

What you’ll bring to the role:

Experience:

5+ years of experience in product management, with a focus on AI or machine learning products.
Experience working in a media, technology, or data-driven organisation.
Experience leading cross-functional teams and managing complex projects.
Demonstrated experience bringing AI products from conception to launch.

Skills:

Deep understanding of AI and machine learning concepts and technologies.
Strong product vision and strategic thinking, with the ability to translate business objectives into actionable product roadmaps.
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence and collaborate with diverse teams.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with a data-driven approach to decision-making.
Understanding of data governance, privacy, and ethical considerations related to AI.Familiarity with agile product development methodologies.

Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, or a related field.
Masters or PhD in AI/ML is highly desirable.
Certifications in AI product management or machine learning are advantageous.
Additional Information

How we work

At Nine, our flexible work options vary by role and team. Depending on the position, this may include flexible hours, hybrid work, or part-time arrangements. We welcome discussing your flexibility needs during the hiring process - just ask the Talent Acquisition team. 

Our employee benefits include:

18 weeks paid parental leave with no distinction between primary and secondary carers.
Access to 'Employee Exclusives' program - a way of getting closer to our incredible brands, offering unique experiences, behind-the-scenes access, and awesome perks.
Digital newspaper subscription to our mastheads.
Annual gift voucher for Stan subscription.

More info at Nine Careers.

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion:

We're committed to a safe, respectful and inclusive Nine. From day one, you'll be encouraged to bring your whole self to work and will be supported to perform at your best.

We encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, people with disabilities, and of all ages, genders, nationalities, backgrounds and cultures as we recognise the importance and value of diverse perspectives. Should you require any adjustments to the recruitment process, please advise us when you apply.

Work rights: Please note to apply for this role you must already have the right to lawfully work and live in Australia.

ABOUT TL CONSULTING




At TL Consulting, we simplify modern complexity with advanced, sustainable engineering. As a proudly Australian-owned consultancy with over 15 years of experience, we empower businesses to innovate and achieve measurable outcomes through scalable, cloud-native solutions. Our 50+ consultants operate across key sectors including banking, financial services & insurance (BFSI), government, retail, education, and entertainment. This cross-industry expertise allows us to deliver customer-centric solutions that combine deep technical capability with a clear understanding of each client’s strategic vision. Our approach leverages proven, scalable blueprints tailored to your business direction- enabling accelerated outcomes, maximized ROI, and sustainable long-term adoption.




We maintain strategic partnerships with organizations that share our vision for a cloud-native future. Through our collaborations with Microsoft, GitHub, and Databricks, we ensure our capabilities stay aligned with evolving best practices. These partnerships also provide access to innovation programs, co-funding opportunities, and dedicated support—helping us accelerate time-to-value for our clients.




ABOUT THE ROLE

The AI Engineering Practice Lead is responsible for building and leading the organisation’s AI Engineering capability, focusing on AI-driven software development, developer productivity, and enterprise AI integration.

This role combines technical leadership, practice development, and delivery oversight, ensuring the organisation can successfully design, implement, and scale AI-enabled engineering solutions such as GitHub Copilot adoption, Model Context Protocol (MCP) integrations, AI developer tooling, and AI-assisted software delivery platforms.

The Practice Lead defines service offerings, develops technical standards, mentors engineers, and supports strategic client engagements.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Practice Leadership

Build and lead the AI Engineering practice within the Cloud & DevSecOps business unit.

Define the practice vision, roadmap, and service offerings aligned with market demand.

Establish technical standards, frameworks, and reference architectures for AI-enabled engineering.

Develop reusable accelerators, patterns, and assets to support delivery at scale.

Grow the capability through recruitment, training, and mentoring of AI engineers.




AI Engineering & Developer Productivity: Lead initiatives focused on AI-assisted software engineering, including:

Enterprise adoption of AI coding assistants

AI-driven developer productivity platforms

AI-enhanced CI/CD workflows

AI agents supporting engineering tasks

Integration of LLMs into developer workflows

Model Context Protocol (MCP) integrations

AI-enabled developer platforms and internal tools




Client Engagement & Delivery:

Act as the technical lead for AI engineering engagements.

Support solution architecture and design for AI-enabled engineering platforms.

Provide technical guidance to delivery teams across projects.

Ensure best practices in AI governance, security, and responsible AI adoption.

Contribute to complex architecture decisions involving AI platforms and developer tooling.




Pre-Sales & Thought Leadership

Support business development and pre-sales activities for AI engineering opportunities.

Collaborate with sales and consulting teams to shape AI-focused solutions and proposals.

Represent the organisation as a subject matter expert in AI-assisted engineering.

Contribute to industry thought leadership, internal knowledge sharing, and practice development.







SKILLS & EXPERIENCE




Required Skills and Qualifications:

Education: Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Technology, or a related field. A Master’s degree or relevant certifications are a plus.




Experience:

10+ years of experience in consulting, system integration, or IT project management.

Strong experience in several of the following areas:

AI-assisted software development tools

CI/CD platforms and DevOps practices

Cloud-native architectures

Developer platforms and internal tooling

Leadership Experience

Technical Skills: Strong experience in several of the following areas:

AI-assisted coding platforms (e.g. GitHub CoPilot)

Large Language Models (LLMs)

Prompt engineering

Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

AI agents and tool use

Experience delivering enterprise technology consulting engagements.

Experience working with senior client stakeholders and technical teams.

WHY JOIN US

Be part of a dynamic consultancy firm that values innovation, growth, and professional development.

Lead a growing practice at the forefront of data-driven business transformation.

Work with diverse, top-tier clients across multiple industries, delivering impactful solutions.

Competitive salary and benefits package.

Career development and growth opportunities within a rapidly expanding organization.




This is a Sydney based, permanent opportunity and will be hybrid in nature. No agencies please. Candidates must have valid visa with full work rights in Australia. Visas will not be transferred or sponsored.

Location: Sydney (Greater Sydney Area)

Job Title: Secure Network Engineering | L3 & Specialist Roles | Canberra
Ad Text:
We are headhunting for three L3/Specialist roles supporting Federal Gov, Aviation, and Defence. These are hybrid, BAU focused roles in "Protected" environments. Whether you are a Cisco DC expert , a Juniper/Automation enthusiast , or a carrier-grade MPLS specialist, I'd love to chat! Active NV1/NV2 required.

The Opportunity:
We are currently headhunting for three distinct, high-impact Network Engineering roles based in Canberra. These are hybrid, BAU operations positions supporting high-profile Federal Government, Aviation, and Defence accounts. If you are looking for a move into a high-security environment with genuine variety and "high-context switching," we have three unique paths available.

Path 1: Cisco DC & F5 Specialist (Global MSP)
Focus on Cisco Nexus (Data Centre) and F5 Load Balancing (LTM/GTM).
Manage WAN, LAN, and DC Routing and Switching across protected infrastructure.
Requires an active, non-lapsed NV1 clearance.


Path 2: Juniper & Automation Specialist (Global MSP)
Focus on Juniper Routing and Switching (R/S) and Fortigate firewalls.
Opportunity to work with automation technologies (Ansible, Jenkins, and Python).
NV1 clearance is preferred, but the client is open to sponsoring a Baseline to NV1 upgrade for the right candidate.


Path 3: Technical Specialist (Secure Ops, Tier 1 Telco)
Support Defence operations with a focus on Enterprise LAN/WAN and Satellite gateways.
Work with carrier-grade MPLS and BGP based networks providing Layer 2 and 3 services.
Requires an active NV1 or NV2 security clearance.



Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples are encouraged to apply.

To apply please click apply or call Cody Berry on 02 8289 3123 for a confidential discussion.

Location: Canberra, ACT
Duration: Initial contract to 30 June 2026 + 12-month extension
Work Arrangement: Hybrid (2–3 days onsite)
Security Clearance: Baseline preferred or minimum AU citizen who can obtain a Baseline clearance

Closing date: Tuesday, 31 March 2026


About the Role

We are seeking an experienced AI Business Analyst to support the delivery of AI-enabled products and services within a Federal Government environment.

This role sits within a multi-disciplinary Agile team, where you’ll bridge business needs and AI/data capabilities—translating complex problems into clear, testable requirements while ensuring solutions are ethical, transparent and compliant.

Key Responsibilities

You will:

Elicit, analyse and document business, data and AI requirements

Translate requirements into epics, features and user stories with clear acceptance criteria

Define AI-specific non-functional requirements (e.g. transparency, privacy, robustness, security)

Map business processes and decision points to determine AI vs rules-based approaches

Support design of AI evaluation frameworks (performance, drift, fairness)

Collaborate with data scientists, engineers and product owners to shape AI solutions

Lead workshops, discovery sessions and backlog refinement activities

Develop test cases for AI/ML features, including A/B testing and validation approaches

Document AI guardrails, prompt design and safety constraints

Support governance activities including privacy, risk and compliance assessments

Contribute to change management, communications and user adoption initiatives

About You

You will bring:

3+ years’ experience as a Business Analyst or Systems Analyst in Agile environments

Hands-on experience delivering AI-enabled solutions (e.g. ML, NLP, LLMs, Copilot)

Strong ability to write testable user stories and acceptance criteria for AI features

Understanding of responsible AI principles (privacy, explainability, bias mitigation, human-in-the-loop)

Experience facilitating workshops and engaging diverse stakeholders

Proficiency with Azure DevOps, Miro (or similar tools)

Excellent communication skills and ability to influence decision-making

Why Apply?

Work on cutting-edge AI initiatives within Federal Government

Contribute to ethical and responsible AI delivery

Collaborative Agile environment with multidisciplinary teams

Flexible hybrid working arrangements

Apply Now

If you’re an experienced Business Analyst with a passion for AI and emerging technologies, apply now.

For a confidential discussion, feel free to reach out via email: zylene_musico@data3.com.au

Drive innovation across our Product Information & Asset Management capabilities 
Foster a high-performance, inclusive, and collaborative team environment
Surry Hills (Sydney based) with a Hybrid working model

 

 

We are Woolworths Group

We are Woolworths Group. 200,000+ bright minds, passionate hearts and unique perspectives connected by a shared Purpose – ‘to create better experiences together for a better tomorrow. It’s that Purpose that fuels our ambition to explore new ideas, make brave commitments and innovate better ways to meet the food and everyday needs of more than 24 million customers every week.




If you’re excited to turn today’s blue sky thinking into a better tomorrow for future generations, you’ll find yourself supported and enriched in a dynamic, inclusive and empowering workplace that reflects the diverse communities we serve. With a culture of genuine care, a flexible approach to work and opportunities across the group to grow your career and make a meaningful impact, the possibilities for what we can achieve together are endless.

 

 

What you'll do

As the Engineering Manager for Product Data Services (PDS), you will lead a mission-critical squad of engineers and QAs. You will be the custodian of our "Source of Truth" for product data, overseeing the platforms that power our entire e-commerce ecosystem. Your role is to build and guide a team to deliver high-quality, scalable, and secure SaaS-style platforms.

You will act as a product-minded technical leader, partnering closely with the Platform Product Lead, Principal Engineer, Architect and Technical BAs to translate a complex data strategy into seamless user-centric features. Your leadership will balance rapid delivery with a relentless focus on technical and architectural excellence.

People Leadership: Manage, mentor, and coach a squad of engineers and QAs, fostering a culture of professional growth, high morale (VoT), and psychological safety.
Technical Strategy & SaaS Mindset: Partner with the Principal Engineer and Product Lead to define a technical roadmap that treats our data services as a scalable SaaS platform, capable of serving multiple business units across the Group.
PIM & DAM Excellence: Oversee the technical implementation and evolution of our PIM and Digital Asset Management (DAM) systems, ensuring robust data ingestion and seamless distribution.
Delivery & Value Realization: Drive the successful delivery of features through "slicing" and iterative releases, ensuring the team builds for scale, reliability, and early business value.
Agile Advocacy: Actively champion agile principles, ensuring "Jira hygiene," efficient sprint cadences, and a fast-paced environment of continuous delivery.
Operational Excellence: Champion engineering best practices, including automated testing, CI/CD, and cyber security (zero vulnerabilities). You will be accountable for the stability and cost-efficiency of the PDS technical stack.
Cross-Functional Collaboration: Foster close ties with the Search and Pricing/Promo squads to ensure a cohesive platform experience across the sub-domain.

 




What You’ll Bring

As a proven Engineering Leader, you will have a minimum of 10+ years of experience in software development, with a track record of leading high-performing teams in a complex, data-heavy environment.

Domain Expertise: Experience with Product Information Management (PIM), Master Data Management (MDM), or Digital Asset Management (DAM) platforms is highly advantageous
Technical Depth: A passionate engineer with strong computer science fundamentals and the ability to guide complex system designs involving data pipelines and high-availability APIs.
Platform Thinking: Demonstrated experience building services with a "platform-as-a-service" mindset—focusing on reusability, multi-tenancy, and scalability.
Tech Stack: Comfort with modern technologies; experience with .Net, Cloud Infrastructure (GCP/Azure), GraphQL and Kubernetes is essential.
Leadership Maturity: Proven ability to improve team health (VoT), mentor senior talent, and navigate technical transitions (like cloud migrations or platform replacements).
Agile & DevOps: Expert understanding of the SDLC, automated testing, and a "You Build It, You Run It" operational philosophy.
Innovation: A curious mindset with an interest in AI-assisted development and how emerging tech can improve engineering productivity and data quality.

 




What You’ll Experience

We embrace diversity of thought and love to take on challenges with a pragmatic and innovative approach. Our culture thrives on mutual respect, care, curiosity, openness, and a proactive attitude. We continually seek ways to pair, collaborate, support, and learn from each other as a team!
Team discounts across our range of Woolworths Group brands you know and love and a robust rewards program that celebrates and incentivises purpose-driven work.
A global business with endless career possibilities around every corner and across every discipline – with valuable exposure to a vast and exciting business network.
A progressive and flexible ‘work from anywhere' policy that gives you the opportunity to harmonise work, life and your wellbeing.
A range of programs to help you prioritise and manage your wellbeing, including 24/7 access to the Sonder app.

 




Everyone belongs at Woolworths Group  

As one of the largest employers in Australia and New Zealand, we aim to create a truly inclusive workplace where everyone feels that they belong, can be their best selves, and reach their full potential.
Diversity, equity, inclusion, and belonging are key to realising our purpose of better together for a better tomorrow. We recognise the value our team’s diversity brings to our business, customers, and communities and that teams with diverse experiences and backgrounds enrich our group and are better able to innovate and solve problems. 
Woolworths Group is an inclusive, team-first company and we value all skills and experiences. If you meet some, but not all of the requirements, we encourage you to submit your application.

Company: Kala Data

About Kala

Kala Data is building energy-backed compute infrastructure by colocating modular data centres with low-cost power generation. We currently operate multiple sites running high-density compute workloads and are transitioning these into GPU-based infrastructure for AI and high-performance computing.

Our core thesis is that energy is local, compute is global — and by controlling power, we can deliver materially lower-cost compute.

Kala is a funded business with active deployments and expansion underway, providing a strong foundation to scale both infrastructure and platform capabilities.

The Opportunity

We are looking for an engineer to build the next stage of our platform — a system that allows customers to access GPU compute through Kala, while dynamically provisioning capacity across both Kala-owned infrastructure and third-party providers.

This is not a typical SaaS role. You will be building a thin orchestration layer over distributed GPU supply, solving real-world infrastructure problems across energy, compute, and networking.

What You’ll Be Building

You will lead the development of a platform that:

Accepts customer requests for GPU compute (via API and/or simple UI)
Routes workloads to available Kala-owned GPU infrastructure
Dynamically provisions external GPU capacity when local supply is constrained (e.g. via third-party APIs)
Abstracts away infrastructure differences so users experience a single, unified platform
Tracks usage, performance, and cost across multiple compute providers

Over time, this system will evolve into a fully integrated GPU platform combining owned and third-party capacity.

Key Responsibilities

Design and build the core job routing and scheduling layer
Integrate with external GPU providers via API (provisioning, monitoring, teardown)
Build a clean abstraction layer for compute resources
Develop APIs for users to request and manage workloads
Implement monitoring, logging, and cost tracking across infrastructure
Work closely with the founders on product direction and system design

What We’re Looking For

Strong backend engineering experience (Python, Go, or similar)
Experience building APIs and distributed systems
Familiarity with cloud infrastructure (AWS, GCP, or similar)
Experience with containers (Docker) and ideally orchestration (Kubernetes)
Comfortable working with infrastructure-level problems (networking, compute, resource allocation)
Ability to operate independently and make architectural decisions

Nice to have:

Experience with GPU workloads or ML infrastructure
Experience with job schedulers (e.g. Slurm, Kubernetes jobs)
Familiarity with marketplace or multi-provider systems

Why This Role

You will be building the core system that underpins the business
Direct exposure to energy + compute infrastructure (not just cloud abstraction)
Opportunity to shape architecture from day one
High ownership and autonomy

Compensation

Competitive salary + equity
Flexible depending on experience

Do you want a job that looks different every day? Have you got an unquenchable passion for data and problem solving? Are you ready to jump into a work environment where you'll collaborate with some of the sharpest minds in the industry? We've got a career you'll enjoy.

Whether you've worked primarily in SQL Server, Postgres, or a more modern cloud-based warehouse, all we really care about is that you have a strong understanding of building data pipelines and are ready to keep learning, every single day.

Please be advised that this role is required to be located in the InterWorks Melbourne office in Melbourne, VIC. Remote work or telecommuting arrangements outside of this jurisdiction are not permissible for this position.

Valid work visa or Australian work rights are required for this position.

Salary range commensurate with experience and qualifications: $100,000-$150,000 AUD

What You'll Do

Tackle diverse projects ranging in duration from a few days to a few months, for clients ranging from local Perth businesses to global enterprises and ASX-listed companies

Work with disparate data sources (relational databases, flat files, Excel, HDFS/Big Data systems, high-performance analytical databases, etc.) to unify client data

Collaborate closely with users to understand their unique needs and support them with the best solutions

Create ETL processes based on client needs while managing client expectations

What You'll Need

Must-Haves

Excellent SQL fluency

Strong ETL proficiency using GUI-based tools or code-based patterns

Understanding of data-modelling principles

Passion for delivering compelling solutions that exceed client expectations

Excellent verbal and written communication skills

Strong problem-solving skills

A genuine thirst to learn

What We'd Like You to Have

Experience with modern data-engineering practices and frameworks

Experience with integration from semi-structured file and API sources

Familiarity with tools such as Informatica (IDMC), Matillion, Fivetran, or dbt

Experience with cloud platforms such as Snowflake, Databricks, Amazon Redshift, Google BigQuery, or Azure Synapse

What We Offer:

Health Benefits: Our people are our most important asset. That's why we offer an exceptional health insurance subsidy through BUPA, ensuring you and your family are well taken care of.

Wellness Benefits: We want you to stay healthy! Our employees receive an annual allowance to spend on their favourite gym memberships, sports activities, or workout apps.

Work-Life Balance: We offer a life balance allowance, encouraging our employees to relax and recharge — whether with family or going solo.

Tech Choice: Windows or Mac? 13-inch or 15-inch? Lightweight or heavy-duty? You know what works best for you. Our employees have a wide choice of equipment, plus a contribution towards standing desk equipment.

Professional Growth: We invest in our people. You'll have access to ongoing training, certifications, and professional development opportunities to keep your skills sharp and your career moving forward.

Community Impact: We InterWorkers like to use our powers for good. As well as undertaking pro bono consulting work, we support our colleagues' individual charitable passions with dedicated time off for volunteering.

Superannuation: Compulsory superannuation contributions are paid on top of your base salary in accordance with Australian legislation.

Why InterWorks

InterWorks is a people-focused tech consultancy that empowers clients with customized, collaborative solutions, and we love pursuing innovation alongside people who inspire us. Our approach to work and community is unique and unconventional—just like us—and that's the way we want it. The only thing missing is you. At InterWorks, we value unique contributions, our people are the glue that holds our business together. We're always looking for the right people, and we could be your perfect fit.

Help shape what’s next in AI deliveryAt Altis, we’re focused helping clients move beyond AI experimentation and into practical, production-ready outcomes

We’re looking for an AI Engineer to join our team, working across the full AI and machine learning solution lifecycle from problem definition and design through to build, deployment, and optimisation.

This is more than a technical delivery role. We’re looking for someone who can combine strong engineering capability with sound judgement, commercial awareness, and a modern advisory mindset. Someone who can work closely with clients, understand real business challenges, and translate them into effective AI and ML solutions that are scalable, governed, and built for the real world.

What you’ll be doing

In this role, you’ll work with clients and internal teams to design, build, and deliver AI and machine learning solutions across a range of use cases and environments.

Your role:

Design, build, test, and deploy AI and ML solutions across the full lifecycle, from proof of concept through to production implementation
Apply modern AI/ML engineering practices, including data preparation, feature engineering, model and prompt development, deployment, monitoring, and optimisation
Translate business problems into practical AI and ML solutions, working directly with client data, technology, and business stakeholders
Embed responsible AI and governance controls into solution design and delivery, aligned to client risk, compliance, and ethical requirements
Contribute technical credibility to conversations around AI strategy, architecture, and governance
Support proposals, scoping, and capability assessments as part of business development activities
Develop reusable artefacts, patterns, and documentation to enable consistent and scalable delivery across engagements
Share knowledge and help uplift AI capability across the broader Altis team
Contribute to Altis AI intellectual property, thought leadership, and external market positioning as opportunities arise

What we’re looking for

We’re looking for someone who brings strong technical depth, end-to-end delivery experience, and the ability to work confidently with clients in complex environments.

What you ideally bring:

5+ years’ experience designing and delivering solutions in a commercial or consulting environment
Proven experience taking AI/ML solutions end-to-end, from problem definition and proof of concept through to production deployment and ongoing optimisation
The ability to assess a client problem and recommend the right approach, whether that is traditional machine learning, generative AI, or a combination of both
Experience working directly with clients and engaging with senior business and technology stakeholders
Strong communication skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts in clear, practical business language
A strong understanding of responsible AI, including governance, risk, ethical considerations, and challenges such as hallucination, bias, and data privacy

Technical capability

You’ll bring strong hands-on capability across modern AI engineering practices and tooling, including:

Strong Python skills
Experience with data preparation, feature engineering, model development, and evaluation
Practical experience building LLM-powered solutions, including prompt engineering, retrieval augmented generation (RAG), vector databases, and agentic patterns such as tool use and multi-step reasoning
Familiarity with LLM APIs and orchestration frameworks, including context management, token economics, and cost optimisation
Experience with MLOps practices, including experiment tracking, model deployment, and performance monitoring in production
Familiarity with REST API design and integration patterns to connect AI solutions with client systems
Experience with AWS and/or GCP, containerisation, and CI/CD pipelines
Exposure to large-scale data processing, streaming, and workflow orchestration tools

Additional requirements

Regular domestic travel will be required to support client delivery and business development


The mindset that matters

Technical strength is essential, but it is only part of the picture.

We’re also looking for someone who brings:

A practical, solutions-focused approach
Strong judgement and the ability to navigate ambiguity
Curiosity and a genuine interest in emerging AI technologies
A collaborative mindset and willingness to share knowledge
The confidence to engage senior stakeholders and provide credible recommendations
A commitment to quality, responsible delivery, and continuous improvement
